task_completion.description: Navigate to the WordPress Widgets configuration area.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the WordPress admin dashboard with 'Appearance > Widgets' selected, or the WordPress Customizer open to the 'Widgets' section.

task_completion.description: Remove all existing widgets from the 'Sidebar' area.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Sidebar' widget area is shown empty before adding new widgets, or the final state shows ONLY the three requested widgets with no pre-existing default widgets.

task_completion.description: Add the 'Product Search' widget as the first item.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The WooCommerce 'Product Search' widget (or block) is visible as the first item at the top of the 'Sidebar' area.

task_completion.description: Add the 'Filter Products by Price' widget as the second item.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The WooCommerce 'Filter Products by Price' widget (or block) is visible as the second item in the 'Sidebar' area, directly below 'Product Search'.

task_completion.description: Add the 'Product Categories' widget as the third item.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The WooCommerce 'Product Categories' widget (or block) is visible as the third item in the 'Sidebar' area, directly below the price filter.

task_completion.description: Enable the 'Show product counts' setting for the 'Product Categories' widget.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The settings panel for the 'Product Categories' widget is expanded, and the checkbox/toggle for 'Show product counts' is visibly checked or enabled.
The 'Title' fields for all three widgets are left blank or at their default values (no custom text typed in), and the 'Update' or 'Publish' button is clicked to save the configuration.

integrity.description: The agent used the WordPress admin GUI to configure widgets.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the WordPress web interface (mouse clicks, drag-and-drop, or block editor usage) rather than a terminal window running wp-cli commands.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with a real web browser.
integrity.visual_evidence: Standard browser UI elements (address bar, tabs) are visible showing the WordPress admin URL, not a text editor or mocked HTML page.

integrity.description: No other widget areas were modified.
integrity.visual_evidence: Other widget areas (e.g., Footer 1, Footer 2, Below Header) remain collapsed or untouched throughout the trajectory.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target sidebar is the primary 'Sidebar' (internal ID: sidebar-1) for the Storefront theme. The required widgets correspond to WooCommerce's 'Product Search' (woocommerce_product_search), 'Filter Products by Price' (woocommerce_price_filter), and 'Product Categories' (woocommerce_product_categories). Ensure the agent configures these specific WooCommerce widgets and not generic WordPress search or category widgets.
